title: Ancient and Imperial China
description: Practice your skills with a quiz on Ancient and Imperial China

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question. 

>"Humane-ness may  make one shed tears and be reluctant to apply  penalties, but law makes it clear that such penalties must be applied. The ancient kings allowed law to be supreme and did not give in to their tearful longings. Hence it is obvious that humane-ness cannot be used to achieve order in the state."

From _Selections from the Han Feizi,_ c. 230 BCE 

**The ideas expressed in the excerpt most directly challenged which prevailing ideology in imperial China?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Read the passage below and answer the question. 

>"The Master said: Guide people with policies and align them with punishments, and the people will evade them and have no shame. But guide them with virtue and align them with li [good customs], and the people will have a sense of shame and fulfill their roles."

From _The Analects of Confucius_, c. 206 BCE–220 CE

**The ideas expressed in the excerpt most directly challenged which of the following ideologies in imperial China?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Read the passage below and answer the question. 

>"In the provinces the salt, iron, and liquor monopolies, and the system of  equitable marketing,  have been established to compete with the people for profit, dispelling  rustic  generosity  and teaching  the people greed. Therefore those who pursue primary  occupations [like farming]  have grown few, and those following  secondary occupations [like trading]  have grown numerous. 

>As strategy increases, basic  simplicity declines; and as the secondary  occupations flourish, those in primary occupations suffer . . . When the people are sincere then there will be sufficient wealth and goods, but when they become extravagant then famine and cold will follow."

From _A record of debate in the court of the Han Emperor Zhao_ in 81 BCE

**The ideas about economic policy expressed in the excerpt could best be used as evidence for the influence of which of the following belief systems?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Read the passage below and answer the question. 

>"Practice no-action;
Attend to doing-nothing;
Taste the flavorless,
Examine the small,
Multiply the few,
Return love for hate."

From the writings of Lao Tzu c. 4th century BCE

**The religion represented in this excerpt's ideas *most directly challenged* which of the following ideologies?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Read the passage below and answer the question. 

>"A young man should serve his parents at home and be respectful to elders outside his home. He should be earnest and truthful, loving all, but become intimate with humaneness. After doing this, if he has energy to spare, he can study literature and the arts."

From _The Analects of Confucius_, c. 206 BCE–220 CE

**The ideas about a young man’s role expressed in the excerpt could best be used as evidence for the practice of which of the following during the Han Dynasty?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Read the passage below and answer the question. 

>"Wu Meng  of  the Jin dynasty  was eight years old and served his parents with extreme filiality. The family  was poor, and their bed had no mosquito net. Every  night in summer many mosquitoes bit him, gorging  on his blood. But despite their numbers he did not drive them away, fearing that they  would go and bite his parents. This is the extreme of  love for parents."

Selection from _The Twenty‑four Exemplars  of Filial  Piety_, c. 1260–1368 C.E.

**This passage, written during the Yuan Dynasty, demonstrates what historical continuity from Han China?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The influence of legalism on government
The influence of Daoism on culture
The influence of Buddhism on social structures
The influence of Confucianism on social structures

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question. 

>"Mang asked what filial piety is. The Master said, "It is being obedient." Soon after, as Fan Chi was driving him, the Master told him, "Mang asked me what filial piety is, and I answered him 'being obedient.'" Fan Chi asked, "What exactly did you mean?" The Master replied, "That parents, when alive, should be served according to ritual; that, when dead, they should be buried according to ritual; and that they should be sacrificed to according to ritual.""

From _The Analects of Confucius_, c. 206 BCE- 220 CE

**The ideas expressed in the passage above most directly contributed to which of the following phenomena in Han China?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 3]]

**Taken together, these two images could best be used as evidence for which of the following historical continuities in China?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Bronze lamp found in second century BCE Han Dynasty-era noblewoman’s grave.
options.caption: Bronze lamp found in second century BCE Han Dynasty-era noblewoman’s grave. Image credit: [Wikimedia](https://cdn.kastatic.org/ka-perseus-images/a22c56220cd78bae9ab05e8e70ee4b3d4261783d.jpg)

options.alt: Bells and other instruments found in a fifth century BCE Warring States Period Chinese nobleman’s grave.
options.caption:
Bells and other instruments found in a fifth century BCE Warring States Period Chinese nobleman’s grave. 
Image credit: [Wikimedia](https://cdn.kastatic.org/ka-perseus-images/d496080ace77790990454ab2eefed9fe6d71e245.jpg)

choices.content: The use of art to support the political power of elites
The spread of trade along the Silk Road
The incorporation of animism into religious practice

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Currency like the one shown in this image would have contributed most directly to which of the following developments in early Imperial China?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question. 

>"Humane-ness may  make one shed tears and be reluctant to apply  penalties, but law makes it clear that such penalties must be applied. The ancient kings allowed law to be supreme and did not give in to their tearful longings. Hence it is obvious that humane-ness cannot be used to achieve order in the state."

From _Selections from the Han Feizi_ by Han Fei, c. 230 BCE 

**Ideas expressed in the passage directly contributed to which of the following developments in Chinese history?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Centralization of authority during the Qin dynasty
Spread of Buddhism into China during the Han dynasty
Political division during the Warring States Period
Development of Confucian ideas during the Zhou dynasty

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question. 

>"With the might of  Qin and the virtues of  Your Highness, in one stroke, like sweeping off  the dust from a kitchen stove, the feudal lords can be annihilated, imperial rule can be established, and unification of  the world can be brought about. This is the one moment in ten thousand ages. If Your Highness allows it to slip away  and does not press the advantage in haste, the feudal lords will revive their strength and organize themselves into an anti‑Qin alliance. Then no one, even though he possess the virtues of  the Yellow Emperor, would be able to annex their territories.”

From _ The Memorial on Annexation  of  Feudal  States_ by Li Si, c. 200 BCE.

**Which of the following developments was this passage reacting to?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The decentralization of authority during the Warring States Period
The centralization of authority during the Qin Dynasty
The development of Legalist ideas during the Warring States Period
The codification of laws during the Qin Dynasty
